statements.content:
> Expanding and collapsing
> Width adjustment
> Fixed area per resolution
Left/right panel can be always shown or expand and collapse depending on user’s needs. Refer to expand and collapse guide.
Users can adjust left/right panel at all times. Width can be adjusted from 300px at default, to minimum 244px to maximum 640px. See the 'Splitter' guide for the details.
Although contents adjust automatically, left/right panel’s width does not change regardless of browser size.
Fixed area per resolution

Summarized informations about displayed contents can be placed on the right panel. Summarized information on contents are shown as default, and when an element is selected, relavant lookup is shown on the right. (When both page summary and lookup is provided at the same time)
Selecting elements to activate lookup
Use the right panel to show rough summary on selected elements.
Show forms for editing or defining properties on page/elements.
Provide list or tree menu on the left panel for user’s to navigate. Relevant contents show up on the content area when selected. Refer to list, tree guide.
CTA button containter 78px

Search option can be used on the left panel when there is need to insert various conditions to view datas or if a user constantly alters conditions to view contents. “Search” or “next” button works as trigger to read data after conditions are inserted. Buttons are placed below all the conditions, and if scroll occurs, CTA button container is fixed to bottom of the screen.
Inserting conditions for navigation

Network element’s physical/logical relations can be easily monitored through data tables, network topology, map, log viewer, service viewer and more.
Place optimized toolbar above viewer’s content area.
Glimpse at network topology view
Summary information within content area can be displayed on top.

Display current menu’s title in standalone products, and in applications, display the title of the content table or map. When adding buttons to page header, being the top most element(with exception to app bar), only place functions that affect the whole page.

Apply tabs when there are multiple menus of the same level while app is running. If there are functions that affect contents, they are placed below tabs.
Refer to tabs guide.
Role as navigation in page transition
When transitioning into detailed screen, create, edit, or add, page header takes the role as page navigation. Refer to page navigation guide.

App bar displays top level informations and actions relevant to the application. It should operate within unified rules and should always be accessible.
(Side navigation will be applied from version 3.5.1 . Check image below.)

Operations that affect the whole application(e.g. notification, global filter, settings, user information, sign out, etc) are placed on the right side of the app bar. Actions can operate through popovers instead of page transition to show/edit informations. Refer to app bar guide.
Tab bar(page header) + Content

Since user’s eye tracks from top-left to bottom-right, Important information should always be placed on top-left. Following images are common screen layouts shared across Samsung’s applications and can be manipulated according to user scenario or patterns.

1. App bar
2. Page header
3. Content area
4. Left panel
5. Right panel
6. Overlay
7. Dialog
Samsung’s applications needs consistant structure maintained. By keeping the same base structure, users can predict interaction patterns without any prior learning.
Last updated: Oct 27, 2025

Layout dashboard 1.0
(New)

• The existing dashboard design guidelines have been reorganized into three refined categories: UI, Card, Responsive.
   - Add Dashboard Card type, usage, behavior
You can open and fold a panel containing data summary information.
You can select the category bar containing the card to open and fold it.

> Expand and collapse
> Scrolling
When the full screen is reduced to a minimum resolution of 1440x700, scrolling through the browser window is displayed.
Display summary information

The data displayed on the dashboard card is abbreviated or emphasized and displayed on the top or right summary panel. In the case of the top summary panel, at least two or more summary data blocks are arranged side by side.
Alarm has increased by 30% compared to yesterday.

Multiple dashboard lists are displayed on the LNB of the dashboard main screen.
You can select a different dashboard from the Dashboard list of LNB, or you can select a different dashboard from the Dropdown list of title Chevron.
If there is an upper folder, place the folder on the LNB and place a breadcrum directly accessible to the upper folder in the content area table together.
Dashboard search (Optional)
You can add a new dashboard. Press Create new Dashboard to enter the edit page of the new dashboard.

There are two ways to add, edit, and delete dashboard cards. You can drag and drop it through the following settings or delete it through the settings. Mandatory cards cannot be hidden and the dashboard is updated only when the Apply button is pressed.
Font can be changed to Defaut, Medium, and Large. The Font size function is placed at the bottom of the card setting dialog.

1. Portal type card setting function
: Place it on the GNB. At this time, the setting icon is placed on the left side of the account icon.
2. Standalone type card setup features
: Place it on the right side of the toolbar. It can be placed inside the 'overflow' icon with other functions. When placed alongside the 'overflow' button, the setting button is placed on the rightmost side.
Setting functions that affect the entire dashboard are placed in the GNB or toolbar according to the site type (Standalone/Portal).

> Card setting
> Multi dashboard
> Display summary information
The dashboard includes 'General Dashboard', whose periodic function is data monitoring, and 'Unified Dashboard', which has customization functions and various view modes.

> There is a managing function with user intervention other than monitoring.
> There is a customization function (e.g., Font Size, Theme color).
> Provides various view modes (e.g., Map view).

> Monitoring is the main purpose.
> Data information inquiry and detailed information can be checked.

1. GNB
2. Card
     2-1. Top Summary (Optional)
     2-2. Right Summary (Optional)
3. Toolbar (Optional)
4. LNB (Optional)
In addition to product logos, accounts, and app switches, features that affect the entire dashboard are placed on the GNB.

It is the space where cards containing information data charts are placed. Cards can be set in various sizes.
Summary information of dashboard data may be displayed in the upper and right areas of the card area.
Data summary information or alarm information is displayed in the upper area of the dashboard.
Display data summary information or alarm information in the right area of the dashboard.
Place the dashboard action function button.
If there are multiple multi-dashboard lists or higher groups, place them on the LNB.
Dashboard is a management tool that visualizes, tracks, and analyzes KPIs, metrics, and key data points to monitor businesses, equipment, and specific processes.

This guide targets on providing proper understanding of resolution and grid system to apply unified system accross products.

Minimum width is 1440px and minimum height is 700px. (Safe area is below 700px, excluding screen’s header and footer area.) 
Although our design guide issues resolution at 1440x1024, we try to keep minimum height on certain component on surpassing 700px.
If browser size becomes smaller than minimum resolution(1440x700), browser scroll shows without altering screen size or position. (min-width : 1440px, min-height : 700px)
If browser size becomes smaller than minimum resolution(1440x700), browser scroll shows without altering screen size or position.
(min-width : 1440px, min-height : 700px)
Browser default scroll bar
If browser size surpasses minimum resolution(1440x700), components change position accordingly. (Refer to following contents.)
A. App bar
B. Root tabs
C. LNB
D. Toolbar

F. Content

F-1. Data table
F-2. Mosaic card
F-3. Topology Viewer
F-4. Map Viewer
F-5. Summary Panel


* Dialog (Modal)
E. Lookup
Height is fixed, and components realign according width change. Refer to app bar guide.

1. Logo : left right padding fixed, left-aligned
2. App Name : left right padding fixed, center-aligned
3. Function button : left right padding fixed, right-aligned
C. LNB,   E. Lookup
Width is fixed, expand with window height, and if header and footer is used, position on top and bottom with fixed height.
Height fixed, width of each tab fixed, left-aligned
1. Root tabs : left-align, overflow when shrunk. Refer to tab guide for further details.
Height is fixed, with components realigning according to width change.

1. Title : left & right padding fixed, left-aligned
2. Function button : left & right padding fixed, right-aligned
F. Content
F-1. Data table
Table width stretches when content area expands. Column width can change with proportion, but certain columns can be fixed.
Cards are left-aligned with a margin of 24px from the left.
F-2. Mosaic card
As the content area expands, if there is space for "one card + default padding value," the card is realigned to fill the space.
F-3. Topology Viewer,   F-4. Map Viewer

Viewer area expands when content area stretches(no change in zoom level or movement in coordinates). Seperate guide should specify how much change in real-estate or ratio each elements take place according to each viewer’s characteristics.
F-5. Summary Panel
Summary panel is positioned above content area.
Panel expands with 24px inner padding and stretches with left alignment when browser window expands.
When screen asset compresses with lookup, summary panel’s width shortens with horizontal scroll.
When dialog size is fixed, they are positioned to the center of the screen.
Minimum resolution (Full-screen modal)

If browser size becomes smaller than minimum resolution(1440x700), display a browser scroll. If browser size is larger than minimum resolution, the footer(CTA button area) must be fixed at the bottom of the viewport.

Theses are the basic principles to create ease of commutication between users and applications(service). Data table, card, viewer’s node information consists of multiple components, and results in different outcomes by hovering or clicking. Increase usability by using these interaction patterns listed below.

> Click
> Right-click
> Hover

> Execute lookup by clicking on items
> Opening detailed screen by link
> Open new window
> Showing charts by toggling on legends
Execute lookup by clicking on items
Selecting on item’s default space opens up lookup(simplified information). For example, selecting table row, card, node icon opens lookup information on the right panel for inspection.
Opening detailed screen by link
Clicking on link opens detailed screen. If there are lots of information or has complicated structure, page navigates instead a popup dialog. If there are more than 3 depth, apply breadcrumb.
Option 1. Open a popup dialog
Option 2. Go to detail page
If menu can to be opened in a new window, icon is shown alongside the menu.
Showing charts by toggling on legends
Hovering on legend makes it become focused. When clicked, only the selected charts are shown. Multiple toggle is allowed and can return to original state by toggling on the legend again.
Unselected charts are dimmed to 30% opacity. Place a focus line around the legend box, and change the curser to hand shape.
Highlight legend and chart when hovering over legend
Corresponding chart is shown.
There is no visible chart that can be highlighted.
When hovering over an unselected legend
Context menu is shown by right-clicking.

1. Overflow menu list when right-clicked on table row. Show more button provides same menu.
2. Right-click on the chart shows context menu about the chart.
3. When clicked on area outside chart or table winthin a card, context menu applicable to card UI is shown. Show more button provides the same menu.

Providing hint on sub-functions

In order to make sure users approach primary functions before the secondary, there should be approximately 0.7 seconds of delay inbetween functions when hovered. Like the application launcher shown below, primary function which is the trigger to launch the application, needs to show 0.7 seconds before showing secondary sub functions like creating categories, or move.

Red-blue palettes can be used to visualize temperature(hot &#8596 cold), signal strength(heat map), and positive/negative values. Colors marked with stars(8 total) must be shown in legends.
Red-blue palettes can be used to visualize temperature(hot
cold), signal strength(heat map), and positive/negative values. Colors marked with stars(8
total) must be shown in legends.
Monochromatic palettes also provide sense of order and is suitable to show relation and trend. When used in light theme, darkest color represents the highest value, whereas in dark theme vice versa.
Sequential color palettes
Sequential color palettes can provide a sense or order. Each consist of color varation from dark to bright colors.
Qualitative color palette
Qualitative color palettes

Categorical color palettes help users to map visual data to meaning instead of numbers. These are made to be visually distinct from each other, and composed with colors that can be differentiated by users with color blindness. UX 3.5 Plus’s color palette has higher saturation compared to UX 3.5.
The color of the text to be on the chart must have a value greater than 5:1 in the WCAG contrast ratio.

> Qualitative color palette
> Sequential color palettes
> Monochromatic palettes
> Diverging color palettes
Following colors represent 6 status colors. Refer to status color guide for more information.
Four colors below are the core colors composing the UI system.
Independent portal (Not linked to ‘Global Launcher’)
In the case of a independent Portal that is not connected to the GL, the existing 3.5 colors can be applied as they are.
Linked to ‘global launcher’
Function app color palette (e.g., User Manager)

Function app
(NFM/NFO/SDN)

Function app
(Vista/NetLiner)

Function app
(Global Launcher Common)
Product color palette (e.g., NFM)

The app launcher color scheme consists of five color groups applied to the NOS Global launcher. Each color reflects the personality and representative features of the app. It is designed to be visually distinct without causing confusion to the user among areas other than Critical, Major, and Minor in the color spectrum.

Based on our Global Launcher product strategy category criteria, Blue represents Domain Controller such as NFM, Green represents ZTP orchestration such as WFM, Pink represents Inventory such as FI, and Purple represents Analytics such as Vista. (If a new product or feature app is added to the Global Launcher, please consult with your design representative.)
Brand Color - Navy 1100, HEX #002280 / RGB (34,128,100)
Product color is the main color that represents our product lineups. Colors are optimized for web environments, and if needed for print, banner, or exhibition, always inquire with design team.

While UX 3.9 color palette focused on delivering brand identity throughout the system, it lacked contrast between colors and wasn’t accessibility friendly in terms of color blind users and prints. UX 3.9 has gone through tonal adjustments to differentiate colors clearly with brightness and saturation levels that results in less eye strain for desk users.

> Product color
> App launcher colors
> UI colors
> Semantic colors
> Chart colors
Colors differentiate brands and make important information stand out. It’s an important tool to create unified user experience across products.

Use only when equal or above 32px.

Font weight allows emphasis on contents and grant hierarchy. To make UI clear, font weight and size should harmonize and should be carefully balanced. Heavy font is always emphasized compare to a same sized font with lighter weight. However, lighter font could be higher up in class depending on size. Although SamsungOne provides variety of sizes, only 200, 400, and 700 should be used on our products. 400 is regular, 700 is bold.

When building system UI, designers and developers must provide the same user experience by using a consistent voice and tone of language.

사용자가 경험하는 네트워크 제품 UI 전반에 차별화된
브랜드 경험을 부여하기 위한 당사 고유의 디자인 철학입니다.
모든 컨텐츠는 삼성전자 네트워크 사업부의 자산이며, 허가되지 않은 배포 또는 복제를 엄격히 금합니다.

Samsung's network operation system has a personality based on business casual tone and manners. As a 5G network management solution based on AI and automation, it must have a tone according to the following three principles in order to give confidence that emergency/failure situations can be prevented and proactive control is possible.

Should provide the network state transparently. Let the user know the detailed information of the element such as when/where the alarm occurred and thr cause of the error using various visual elements. Being transparent and clear implies honest, trustworthy and well-founded.
Communication should always be concise so that messages can be recognized in a short time. By reducing unnecessary explanations, users can make decisions without hesitation.

The voice of the system must deliver the message in a stable tone that can be trusted in any situation. In a careful and calm tone, the system should list the cause-and-effect relationship in the problem and ask for confirmation from the user for the next step. The message can be more descriptive when needed, but it should imply that it respects the user's knowledge.
For clear communication between the system and the user, the voice should be implemented in the following tone.

> Active and passive voice
> Refined formal expression rather than casual expression
> Failure/error/emergency messages
> Guide to complex processes
You can directly guide the user's goals. It is suitable for guiding the user's actions and situations.

“Add a batch command.”
“An administrator is publishing the same project.”
“The selected node has a child node.”
It is suitable for guiding the detected system status or for displaying error messages.

“The database in the table needs to be refreshed to run a simulation.”
“Dashboard view setting is not saved.”
“The maximum number of iterations has been reached.”

When writing strings, active or passive sentences are used in various situations. It cannot be said that a certain way is preferred, and the appropriate method should be used depending on the situation.
Refined formal expression rather than casual expression
The system grammar should be written in refined and intuitive expressions to help the user get done their job. Keep the tone as concise and formal as possible and do not use exclamation marks.

Drag and drop silce templates here
or browse templates.
But don't worry you can easily drag and drop slice templates here or click the browse button below to add.
Failure/error/emergency messages

In the event of a failure/error/emergency, the user should be able to quickly recognize the current status, resolution process, and expected results. The message should consist of relatively implicit and purposeful sentences, so that there are no unnecessary elements for the user to understand the context. Try to express it in as concise sentences as possible.
Sentences that begin with a verb
The verb is placed first so that the user can figure out what to do next from the beginning of the sentence.
“Select batch execution target.”
Include the object in the sentence
If it is a message that guides the user in a complex situation with multiple structures, the object of the user's action should be clearly indicated.
“Delete current event list.”
Provide a solution to the error along with guidance to help the user solve the current situation and move forward.
Failed to connect to the database server. Please contact system administrator to resolve this problem.
Failed to connect to the database server. Please contact

You are not allowed to access this page.
Contact security administrator for permission changes.
You are not allowed to access this page.
Writing the guide from the user perspective
Use the second person (you, your) as often as possible for more engagement.
In the case of how-to guides, rather than showing all the processes at the beginning, briefly describe the key actions that the user must perform at each necessary step.

(Step 1)   “Select target type of batch file.”
(Step 2)   “Select a batch command.”
(Step 3)   “Review the execution result.”
Select target type of batch file.”
Review the execution result.”

For flows that are used intermittently, such as firmware update, rich guides can be helpful. However, the guide text on pages that are used repeatedly can act as visual noise. In this case, hide the message and show it as a tooltip if necessary.
Run the task periodically
Description sample text description sample text
Guide to complex processes

Writing guide for help messages of new task, initial installation, and adding equipment:
The process of adding new network-related project files, templates, node elements, etc. is often a complicated process. Operators at network operation centers usually tools from multiple vendors, so they want to be informed about how to do it. A message about how to perform step by step can be written as a relatively long description.

> Sentence case
> Title case
> All caps

“Click here to audit the imported data.”
“The modification was successful. Please go back to the system registration panel.”
Applies to: Almost every text string used within the app, such as main menu, sub menu, context menu, tab label, header, button label, etc.

Yang Command Manager
Role Manager
Parameter Audit

EMS (Element Management System)
NE (Network Equipment)
RAN (Radio Access Network)
VNF (Virtualized Network Function)
5G FWA ( Wireless Access)
NFVO (Network Functions Virtualization Orchestrator)
“The Group has been successfully deleted from EMS group list.”
Function (general noun) abbreviation
Applies to: Abbreviations for proper nouns such as application name(e.g., COS), specific feature

• Update to the latest components, such as example screens.
   - Change to the latest help icon.
• Voice and Tone guideline has been newly added.

• ‘Voice and tone’ guide has been published in English.
• Changed the capitalization guide to apply the sentence case to almost all strings used within the app except app name and proper nouns.
Voice in writing reflects the identity of our company. With clear and consistent voices, the trust can be built in Samsung products and differentiate its services.
The tone refers to the way our system communicates(speaks) with users. Usability can be improved by providing information in an appropriate tone according to the operating situation.

> Numbers formatted with 0
> Numbers formatted with colons and periods
> Numbers formatted to group digits
> Numbers formatted with currency symbol
> Numbers formatted with unit symbol
> Numbers formatted with unit
> Number formatted as geographic coordinates
> Numbers formatted with hyphens

> Alignments
> Shortened strings
> When there is no value

This document outlines the rules primarily applied in UX3.5, based on international conventions for number formats. The purpose of this document is to convert numeric objects into human-readable strings while ensuring consistent number formatting across our system.
0 indicates the position where a digit is included. A leading zero can either be treated as a space or included as part of the number.
Numbers formatted with colons and periods

When displaying numerical data such as IP addresses, version information, or decimal values, use periods as delimiters without spaces between the numbers and the periods.
※ When denoting version information, it’s common to attach ‘v’ directly to the number (e.g., v1.0). In contrast, ‘ver’ is typically followed by a space before the number (e.g., ver 1.0).
Numbers formatted to group digits
Numbers formatted with currency symbol
Numbers formatted with unit symbol
Numbers formatted with unit
Number formatted as geographic coordinates
Numbers formatted with hyphens

When displaying groups of numbers with large values, prefer to represent them as short strings. When you use the short form, the units are shown as an abbreviation, such as K (thousand), M (million), or B (billion). This approach improves readability and ensures consistency throughout the system.
If a field has no value, use a dash(“–“) instead of “N/A” to improve readability. “N/A” can make it harder to quickly identify missing values in a data table.

source.layerName: If a field has no va

statements.content: • A guide for number formatting has been added. This guide covers the notation of numbers displayed with symbols or units, as well as alignment based on number properties.

In a data table, the alignment of numbers significantly impacts readability. Numbers in table cell should be aligned according to following guideline.

 Right-aligned numbers
1. Currency, amount, and quantity
	• Example: $1,234, ₩56,789
	• Aligning numbers by decimal places makes comparisons easier
2. Decimal numbers and percentages
	• Example: 3.14, 99.9%
	• Keeping decimal points aligned improves readability
3. IDs, codes, and reference numbers
	• Example: 00123, 45678
	• Right alignment helps compare numerical values

 Left-aligned numbers
1. Version numbers
	• Example: v1.2.3, 2.10.4
	• Each segment of the version has independent meaning, so left alignment is more natural
2. Years, numeric dates and time
	• Example: 2023, 2025-03-27, 2025-03-27 23:45:59
	• Left alignment improves readability for timestamp fields, as users scan from the beginning of the string.
3. Phone numbers
	• Example: +82-10-1234-5678
	• The structure (country code, area code) is more important than numerical comparison
4. IP addresses
	• Example: 192.168.0.1
	• Left alignment preserves the logical grouping of segments
5. Alphanumeric data
	• Example: Item123, A42
	• When mixed with text, left alignment is more intuitive
In a data table, the alignment of numbers significantly impacts readability. Numbers in table cell should be aligned according to following guideline.
1. Currency, amount, and quantity
• Aligning numbers by decimal places makes comparisons easier
2. Decimal numbers and percentages
• Keeping decimal points aligned improves readability
3. IDs, codes, and reference numbers
• Right alignment helps compare numerical values
1. Version numbers
• Each segment of the version has independent meaning, so left alignment is more natural
2. Years, numeric dates and time
• Left alignment improves readability for timestamp fields, as users scan from the beginning of the string.
3. Phone numbers
• The structure (country code, area code) is more important than numerical comparison
4. IP addresses
• Left alignment preserves the logical grouping of segments
5. Alphanumeric data
• When mixed with text, left alignment is more intuitive

If a number has more than three digits, grouping the digits can make it easier to read or estimate values such as prices and sizes. The grouping size can be three, four, or any other desired format. However, in UX3.5, it is generally recommended to use groups of three—except for geographic coordinates.

The currency symbol is displayed before the number by default. In most countries, it is placed before the number, but in some European countries and Vietnam, it is common to place it after the number. Therefore, it may be worth considering adjusting the position of the currency symbol based on the market.
As defined in the UX3.5 input field component properties, the symbol is hidden while the user is entering a currency value. Once the input is complete, the symbol reappears before the number. There is no space between the symbol and the number.
The symbol for specific units, such as the % (percent sign), °C/°F (temperature sign), or ° (degree sign), is displayed after the number. There is no space between the symbol and the number.

Abbreviations for units, such as kbps (kilobits per second), MHz (megahertz), and GB (gigabyte), are displayed after the number. To improve readability, a space is applied between the number and the unit.
When displaying geographical information such as latitude and longitude, use the following formats. The directional symbols (N, S, E, W) are displayed after the number with a space.

Use hyphens when formatting dates and phone numbers. The format for displaying phone numbers may vary by country and region, but they are generally displayed using hyphens and parentheses to seperate the country code and area code. Dates are also typically formatted with hyphens. Do not apply spaces between hyphens and numbers.
DMS (Degrees Minutes Seconds)
With the area code set off by parentheses
International Standard (E.164 format)
Last updated: Apr 7, 2025

> Standard notation of dates
> Absolute dates
> Relative dates
> Range indication
Date and time are shown in one format. Times are shown in 24hour format without am/pm, and seconds can be shown if needed.
Standard notation of dates

Applications date and time notation are shown as below in our products.
1. Replace “T” with two spaces to improve legibility.
2. Notation can be changed to prefered format for business areas.
International standard date notation
International standard time notation
International standard date & time notation

YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS
2020-12-31T23:59:59
UX3.5 standard format (w/ double space)
International standard notation
Date notation is as follows.
Show months below 10 in single digit (e.g. 4)
Show months in 2 digits (e.g. 04)
Show months in 3 digit text abbreviation (e.g. Apr)
Show months in full English name (e.g. April)
Show dates below 10 in single digit (e.g. 4)
Show dates in 2 digits (e.g. 04)
Show days in 3 digit abbreviation (e.g. Tue)
Show days in full English name (e.g. Tuesday)

Design guide’s date notation generally follow Group A’s method, but can be adjusted accordingly.
Date notation can be selected depending on the situation or content’s objective. Use long version as a general notation to deliver information clearly. However, short version can be used if there is a lack of space.
, but can be adjusted accordingly.
Date notation can be selected depending on the situation or content’s objective. Use long version as a general notation to deliver information clearly.
However, short version can be used if there is a lack of space.
Standard date notation per country
US, Canada, Korea, Japan, China, Taiwan, Hungary
MMM D (e.g. Apr 1)

M/D/YYYY (e.g. 4/1/2020)
MMMM D, YYYY (e.g. Apr 1, 2020)
*Month names are used in descriptive scripts.
  (e.g. The site list has been updated on April 1, 2020.)
India, UK, Mexico, Germany, Russia, Italy, Netherlands, Vietnam, Iran, France, Spain, Poland, Uzbekistan, Saudi Arabia, Thailand, Indonesia, New Zealand, Australia, Brazil…
, UK, Mexico, Germany, Russia, Italy,
Netherlands, Vietnam, Iran, France, Spain,
Poland, Uzbekistan, Saudi Arabia, Thailand,
Indonesia, New Zealand, Australia, Brazil…
D MMM (e.g. 1 Apr)
Time and date of occurance are shown accurately. Orders are shown in Month/Date/Year with a gap (two spaces) before time.
Date + Time (Hour:Minute:Second)

Future and past times are shown in relevance to currrent time. Days before and after 7 days are shown in absolute dates. Use absolute dates  on functions like export, or print where relative time is meaningless.
Absolute date (e.g., 4/1/2020)

Show dates and times of past events in relative dates. 
Although usefull to visualize recent temporary events, knowing the exact times are difficult so use with caution.
Less than 1 day (from now)
Show reserved or planned date and times in relative time.

When showing range of time or date, use gap in “-”(en-dash) in between.
e.g. 4/1/2020 - 4/2/2020
Last updated: Apr 7, 2025
Date and time formats 1.1
• In addition to global standards, a guide has been added for the formats commonly used in UX3.5.

The mouse cursor serves as an interactive feedback element that intuitively communicates the actions a user can perform at a given location, such as selecting text, clicking buttons, or resizing components. While it generally follows the default cursor style provided by the operating system, context-appropriate cursors should be used to ensure a consistent user experience.
The example images in this guide are based on the default system cursors of Windows 11. Appearance may vary slightly depending on the operating system.
Non-clickable default area
Clickable UI elements such as buttons, tabs, links
Input fields, selectable text areas
Before drag starts (grabbable element)
When moving a draggable element
Use the pointer cursor for clickable elements, and the default cursor for non-clickable elements.
During data loading, use the default cursor, and provide visual feedback on the loading element itself (e.g., spinner, loading text).

If the tooltip includes an interactive action (e.g., a link or button), use the pointer cursor. 
If the tooltip is purely informational with no interaction, use the default cursor.
Description sample text description sample text
Last updated: Jun 27, 2025
Use cursor types in a clear and consistent manner based on interactivity.

> Action 
> Loading
> Tooltips
When an entire row is clickable, show the pointer cursor on hover over the row.
If only specific items within a row are clickable, the row should show the default cursor on hover. Then, only show the pointer cursor when hovering directly over the interactive elements.

Mouse cursor (v 1.0)
(NEW)
• Mouse cursor guideline has been newly added.

Consistent labels for common tasks allow users to predict how they will interact with applications (services). This list contains commonly understood names and terms generally used in the network operation industry and our products. Since this is a recommended variety of terms, for some functions, a different name may be required to accomodate to the corresponding context.

This action adds an existing object to a new context. The action label is marked with ‘Add + object’ to make the function predictable. (In principle, it should be clearly marked with the object as Add OOOO. e.g., Add plan, Add role, Add user, Add target) Use separately from Create.
Compare Create, Insert, New, and Upload.
This action immediately saves the changes to the database without confirmation. In the case of a popup dialog, you can close the popup through this action. Use it separately from Save.
This action is used for stoping the currently running function or when forcibly closing an app.
Compare Cancel and Reset.

This action is applied when providing a route for users to return to the previous step in a flow within a sequence. It is often paired with Next, and is sometimes replaced with Previous. Be careful not to use it in pop-up for simple information inquiry or detail view page.
Compare Previous and Undo.

This action opens a file browser window to import files locally or from a server. Be careful not to apply a custom UI other than the system default browser UI in the scenario of selecting a local file.

This action terminates the current step. If there is an action button (e.g., Apply) on the screen, provision of a Cancel button must be considered. The Cancel button in the pop-up dialog functions to close the dialog and return to the previous step.
Compare Abort, Close and Reset.

This action either deletes all or selected items. Clear is mainly applied to clear the entire items/text only for data that does not affect the system, such as log or notification. It can also be applied when you want to delete all input field values at once, or delete applied filters at once and return to the default state.
Compare Delete and Remove.

This action is used to close the currently viewed page or pop-up dialog. In UX 3.5, Close is often replaced with an ‘X’ icon, and the ‘X’ icon button must be applied in dialogs regardless of whether there is a Cancel button or not. (e.g., close popup dialog, or close Lookup, close Context menu/popover with relatively large content)
See also Cancel and Done.

You can create new items. The action label is marked with ‘Create + object’ to make the function predictable (e.g., Create new plan, Create new group, Create new role). Must be used separately from the Add action used when adding previously existed data to a new context. The newly created item should be placed at the top of the list.
Compare Add, Copy, Insert. See also New.
Users can change existing settings as needed.

This action creates a new instance of the selected item. Action labels are marked with ‘Copy + object’ or ‘Copy to + destination’ (e.g., Copy to clipboard) so that users can clearly recognize the function. Since the Copy action copies an instance to a location specified by the user, it should be used separately from Duplicate, which creates the same object in the original location of the item. In scenarios where the user does not specify a location, the copied object is placed directly above the target item or at the top of the list.
It is used to confirm that the user is aware of the system status or message. Use instead of OK or Yes.

It is an action that confirms that additions/changes of files and folders are permanently stored in the database. While Save refers to the short-term storage of pending information, the Commit action refers to permanent records that cannot be rolled back. When a user commits a change, a part of the head revision of the repository is created, so it must be used separately from the Save action.

This action proceeds a permanent deletion of a item. Unlike Clear or Remove actions, Delete has a permanent meaning and must be accompanied by clear guidance. If the Delete action has a serious impact on the system, it should be clearly labeled with ‘Delete + object’ so that the user can predict the function. In the confirmation window that appears when selecting the delete function, the system impact related to the object to be deleted should be clearly described.
Compare Clear and Remove.
It is an action to place feasible file/files to an environment accessible by the user after the build is completed.

Completes the action on the current page. For most pages, this means completing a task and returning to the previous page. In most cases, ‘Done’ is used alone as a label, and if necessary, there can be considerations to provide an option to check the details of the execution result together.
See also Close and Finish.
This is an action that saves a file/files stored in the cloud system to a local PC. It means to store data as is on the local PC without conversion.
This is an action in which the user moves an item from area A to area B or within the same area using a drag and drop behavior.

This action creates a new instance identical to the selected item in place. It should be used separately from Copy, which copies a new instance to a location specified by the user. The duplicated object is placed directly above the target item.
Compare Copy and Save as.
This function allows user to enter a page (or mode) where the value of an item can be modified. Make the function predictable by clearly marking it as ‘Edit + target’.

It transacts the command to the designated target. Although it is used almost synonymously with the term Run, Execute is often accompanied by the meaning of completion along with execution. Run can be used to start a series of processes such as simulation, and Execute can be used to call and transact a specific command.

It is an action to save data sets from cloud to the local PC. It saves the data table on the screen as it is, or saves a large amount of data as a document file according to the condition value selected by the user without an inquiring on the screen. When performing the export function, a UI that allows the user to specify the file format and storage location should be provided. For the option to save to local PC,  provision of Windows default file browser is neccesary.
Compare Download and Report.
It is an action to narrow down the total amount of data by composing a list with only objects that match the keyword or attribute value specified by the user.
An action that completes a series of steps in a flow with a sequence. It can be used with the Prev and Next buttons, and you can consider using it at the end of a step.

The Hide action can be used to hide specific items in the data list (if there is a region constraint or because it being a supporting information) to show only the necessary parts. Hide should always be paired with a Show that can restore that action. It can be used in conjunction with a chevron button to hint the direction in which the hidden area will appear. It can also be provided as a switch, or text button.

This action loads an external file (either on the server or on the local PC) into the application. The following actions can be performed by outputting the imported data to a table or as an input data. Similar to export, you can consider the UI to import by converting the data format. When importing from a local PC, the Windows default file browser must be provided.
This action adds an element to a specific position among sorted items.
An action that starts a new standalone web page or an app.
It is an action to open and use a file stored in the server.

This action moves the selected item to another container (e.g., folder, page). If the user need to know which element can be moved, use 'Move + object’. If the user needs to know where the selected element can be moved to, apply 'Move to + destination' as a label. (e.g. Move target, Move to Trash)

New is a bigger concept than Create, and can be used for content that needs to be newly created from zero base. When creating a new composition without a template or guide, apply New. Otherwise, see Create or Add. (e.g., New event)
It is an action that calls the proceeding step in a flow with a sequence.
See also Back and Previous
OK can be used when user approval is required. It can be mainly used in the Acknowledgment dialog where only one accept button is given, and can be replaced with Confirm. Yes/No is not used.

This action can be used when you want to preview the contents of a specific configuration before publishing. Preview shows the actual published result in the form of a preview and does not actually affect the system. It should be displayed without departing from the current flow.
This action allows the user to return to a preceeding step in a flow with a sequence. Often paired with Next, in UX 3.5 it is primarily used in the page navigation UI.
This action sends the selected file or data set to print. Because a separate UI for output is neccessary, place elipses at the end of the label.
Reloads the screen when there is information out of sync on the screen.
Compare Restore and Rollback.

This action deletes the selected item from the currently viewed list. Removed data can be recalled at any time. Unlike Delete, this action does not have a significant effect on the system, so it can be defined as a relatively mild action.
Compare Clear and Delete.
This action creates a new account. When creating a new account, an input form must be provided so that ID, password, authority, session time, etc. can be set.

This action restores the most recent value. The most recent value is the last value inputted by the user saved by pressing the Apply button. This action restores the applied data without page transition in the main page or pop-up dialog.
Compare Cancel, Restore default and Undo.
This is an action to restore a file to its original state after moving file to another location, or after actions simillar to Clear and Remove.
Restore defaults can be used when reverting to the default values applied when creating a function for the first time.

It is a recovery action with a larger and more complex concept than Restore. For example, this action can be used to restore the entire version to the previous version while performing system deployment. Use Restore for most simple actions.
Compare Restore and Undo.

This function starts a specified action. Although being used almost synonymously with Execute, which completes the whole process, Run contains the nuance of 'can be stopped or paused after it starts’. Apply Run when starting a process such as simulation. After the Run action, it should be accompanied by feedback that informs the user of the progress.

Stores data on the server, not the PC. Pending modifications to a file or project documentation can be saved. It is an action that saves not only the current page but also all the changed values required for the setting stage. Window or panel should not automatically close when the Save button is clicked.
Compare Apply and Commit.

This is an action that allows users to input keywords or option values into the database and retrieve all matching items. In operation apps where multiple search boxes are often displayed on one page, in which searchable item options should be clearly displayed within the field as a placeholder text (e.g., Search NE, Search user).
This action allows hidden items from Hide action in the data list to be seen. Show action should always be paired with a Hide action.

This action reverts the expanded information area to its original size. If the overflowed content is expanded with the Show more button, the Show more button should be replaced with the Show less button.
See also Show more and Overflow content(pattern guide).
It is an action that temporarily expands the area and calls the truncated information when part of the information is not visible due to spatial constraints.
Sign in is an action in which a user enters credentials and password to enter a site or application, stores them in a database (close to submission), and creates a new session.
This is an action to abort a session created by the user from a site or application that has already been granted access and leave the site.

Changes the ordering of items listed in columns of tables, lists, and topology viewers. If there are many sorting options, it can be provided as a context menu. The selected sort value should be displayed even when the menu list is closed.
This action starts a flow with a sequence.
Compare Execute, Finish, Launch and Run.
This action selects an item from a list.
This action selects all items in a list.
Saves the currently viewed file or project document as a separate new file. Users should be able to specify a name and location for the new file.
Cancels the execution history and returns to the previous step. Although Similar to Revert, Undo can be paired with Redo in certain cases. It can be used repeatedly to proceed to the previous step.
This action changes the version status of the current product to the latest version developed so far or the specified version.
Transfer files from the local PC to the server.
This action may be considered when providing a route to view additional information. Through the View details action, it is possible to move to another page, open a lookup, or open a pop-up dialog.
• Action labels guideline has been newly added.
• This guide has been translated into English and re-uploaded.
Last updated: Dec 17, 2021
